hypothesis: If a convolutional neural network is pretrained on a data-rich domain of additive-free SEM images before fine-tuning on a data-poor domain of additive-containing samples, then it will achieve higher classification accuracy for lithium-ion battery cathode composition and aging state identification compared to models trained without this two-stage transfer learning approach, because pretraining enables the network to learn transferable morphological features that compensate for limited labeled data in the target domain.
